articles.title: Widow of college police officer killed in crash sues Boston music venue, alleges they overserved other driver
articles.description:
Keoma Duarte was heading home from a concert at House of Blues when he allegedly struck and killed Endicott College Police Sgt. Jeremy Cole in 2024.

The post Widow of college police officer killed in crash sues Boston music venue, alleges they overserved other driver appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Keoma Duarte struck and killed Sgt. Jeremy Cole after leaving a concert at House of Blues.
The lawsuit claims the venue overserved alcohol to Duarte before the fatal crash.
This case raises concerns about alcohol service practices at Boston venues and public safety.

articles.title: 3 must-see summer movies coming soon to a theater near you
articles.description:
A Spielberg alien film, a Nolan adaptation of Homer, and a Massachusetts-filmed Anthony Bourdain biopic.

The post 3 must-see summer movies coming soon to a theater near you appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Watch Spielberg's alien film in Boston theaters this summer.
Experience Nolan's adaptation of Homer's work, premiering in June 2026.
See the Anthony Bourdain biopic, filmed in Massachusetts, hitting screens soon.

articles.title: LinkedIn releases top 10 best employers for career growth in Boston
articles.description:
Healthcare, life sciences, and finance companies dominated this year’s list.

The post LinkedIn releases top 10 best employers for career growth in Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: LinkedIn identified top 10 Boston employers for career growth in 2023.
Healthcare, life sciences, and finance sectors dominated the list.
Local job seekers can target these companies for better career advancement.

articles.title: Police looking to identify alleged Beacon Hill jewelry thief
articles.description:
The theft occurred earlier this month, according to Boston police.

The post Police looking to identify alleged Beacon Hill jewelry thief appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston police are investigating a jewelry theft in Beacon Hill earlier this month.
The alleged thief remains unidentified, raising concerns among local jewelers and residents.
Increased police presence may follow to enhance security in the Beacon Hill area.

articles.title: MassDOT and Boston clash over Summer Street closure on World Cup game days
articles.description:
The closure is a “matter of necessity given the expected increase in foot traffic near" South Station, according to MassDOT.

The post MassDOT and Boston clash over Summer Street closure on World Cup game days appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: MassDOT plans to close Summer Street during World Cup game days to manage foot traffic.
Closure affects access near South Station, expected to handle increased crowds on game days.
Boston officials oppose the closure, raising concerns about local business impacts and traffic disruptions.

articles.title: Lucinda Williams on Bob Dylan, her stroke, and how this ‘World’s Gone Wrong’
articles.description:
Ahead of three Mass. shows, one of the NYT’s “Greatest Living American Songwriters” talks about her new protest album.

The post Lucinda Williams on Bob Dylan, her stroke, and how this ‘World’s Gone Wrong’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Lucinda Williams will perform three shows in Massachusetts this month.
Her new album, 'World’s Gone Wrong,' addresses social issues and protests.
Boston residents can expect a powerful message from a celebrated songwriter.

articles.title: Barney Frank, longtime liberal congressman from Mass. and trailblazer for gay rights, dies at 86
articles.description:
After representing broad swaths of Boston's suburbs in Congress for 32 years, Frank and his husband moved to Ogunquit, Maine.

The post Barney Frank, longtime liberal congressman from Mass. and trailblazer for gay rights, dies at 86 appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Barney Frank, former congressman, died at 86, impacting Boston's political landscape.
Frank served 32 years representing Boston's suburbs, advocating for progressive policies.
His legacy includes significant advancements in gay rights, influencing local LGBTQ+ communities.

articles.title: What meteorologists are saying about scattered thunderstorms, when the heat will break in Boston
articles.description:
"Still hot & humid with scattered afternoon thunderstorms expected."

The post What meteorologists are saying about scattered thunderstorms, when the heat will break in Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Scattered thunderstorms expected in Boston this afternoon, bringing temporary relief from heat.
Temperatures remain high, with humidity levels affecting comfort for residents.
Meteorologists predict a significant cooldown by the weekend, improving outdoor conditions.

articles.title: How did the 1994 World Cup go for Boston and Foxborough, and how does it compare with 2026?
articles.description:
Despite some recognizable problems — local organizers initially struggled to raise money, hotel bookings lagged, and certain ticket costs soared — the tournament won over some of its sharpest critics, including Boston Globe columnist Bob Ryan.

The post How did the 1994 World Cup go for Boston and Foxborough, and how does it compare with 2026? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: 1994 World Cup faced funding challenges and low hotel bookings in Boston.
Ticket prices soared, impacting local fans and attendance rates.
Local organizers aim to improve logistics for the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Colorful milk snake found on the loose in the South End
articles.description:
"They’re notorious escape artists."

The post Colorful milk snake found on the loose in the South End appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A colorful milk snake was discovered roaming in Boston's South End neighborhood.
Local residents reported the snake, known for being an escape artist, on May 20, 2026.
Authorities advise residents to stay alert and report any sightings for safety.
